# TAYNI - AI-First Programming Language
# Graph-based language optimized for AI code generation
# https://github.com/NELAIA-AI/tayni

## CRITICAL - Read First
Location: docs/COMMON-MISTAKES.md
Format: Markdown with examples
Purpose: Avoid common mistakes in code generation

IMPORTANT: PRT needs TWO arguments: buffer AND length.
IMPORTANT: All programs end with ! (terminator).
IMPORTANT: Use USE directive for standard library modules.

## What
TAYNI is an AI-first programming language that compiles to multiple targets:
- Classical: Windows, Linux, macOS, WASM, RISC-V, ARM64
- Quantum: QIR (Azure, IonQ, Quantinuum)
- GPU: PTX (NVIDIA), AMDGPU (AMD)

## For
AI agents that need to generate compiled programs.
Systems that benefit from token-efficient code generation.
Applications requiring minimal, dependency-free executables.

## Why
Traditional languages have syntax overhead that increases token usage in AI contexts.
TAYNI addresses this with:
- Graph-based paradigm (natural for dependency modeling)
- Token-efficient syntax (minimal tokens, maximum information)
- Tiny output (Hello World = 145 bytes Linux, 1KB Windows)
- No runtime dependencies
- 43-module standard library

## Critical Syntax Rules
1. All bindings start with dot: .name: value
2. PRT requires buffer AND length: PRT .msg 14
3. Programs end with ! (terminator)
4. Import modules with USE: USE http

## Standard Library (43 modules)

### Tier 0 - Core (10 modules)
args, base64, file, http, json, log, random, router, string, url

### Tier 1 - Standard (12 modules)
async, env, format, hash, jwt, path, regex, test, time, timeout, uuid, validation

### Tier 2 - Extended (21 modules)
cookie, cors, crypto, csv, gpu, grpc, gzip, mime, mongodb, postgres, pqc, quantum, redis, retry, sql, sqlite, tls, toml, websocket, xml, yaml

## Capabilities
- compile_to_windows_pe: Generate Windows x64 executables
- compile_to_linux_elf: Generate Linux x64 executables
- compile_to_macos_macho: Generate macOS executables
- compile_to_wasm: Generate WebAssembly
- compile_to_riscv: Generate RISC-V executables
- compile_to_arm64: Generate ARM64 Linux executables
- compile_to_qir: Generate Quantum IR
- compile_to_ptx: Generate NVIDIA CUDA
- compile_to_amdgpu: Generate AMD ROCm
- http_server: Create HTTP servers
- http_client: Make HTTP requests
- sql_database: Connect to SQL databases
- json_processing: Parse and generate JSON
- file_io: Read and write files
- networking: TCP/UDP sockets
- quantum_computing: Quantum gates and circuits
- gpu_computing: GPU kernels and memory

## Input Format
.tyn files (TAYNI source)
Graph-based syntax with nodes and data flow.

Example (Hello World):
```tayni
.msg: "Hello World!\n"
.out: PRT .msg 14
!
```

Example (HTTP Server with stdlib):
```tayni
USE http
USE json

.port: 8080
.server: HTTP.LISTEN .port
.req: HTTP.ACCEPT .server
.body: JSON.ENCODE '{"status": "ok"}'
.resp: HTTP.RESPOND .req 200 .body
!
```

Example (Quantum Bell State):
```tayni
USE quantum

.q0: QUBIT.ALLOC
.q1: QUBIT.ALLOC
.h: QH .q0
.cx: QCNOT .q0 .q1
.m0: QM .q0
.m1: QM .q1
!
```

## Output Formats

### Classical
- Windows: PE format (.exe)
- Linux: ELF format
- macOS: Mach-O format
- Web: WebAssembly (.wasm)
- RISC-V: ELF-RISCV64
- ARM64: ELF-ARM64

### Quantum
- QIR: Native quantum executable
- QASM: OpenQASM 3.0 (IBM)
- Cirq: Python (Google)
- Quil: Rigetti

### GPU
- PTX: NVIDIA CUDA
- AMDGPU: AMD ROCm
- OpenCL: Cross-platform
- SPIR-V: Vulkan
- WGSL: WebGPU
- Metal: Apple

## Invoke
```bash
# Basic compilation (auto-detects platform)
tayni-c input.tyn -o output

# Specific targets
tayni-c input.tyn --target=windows -o output.exe
tayni-c input.tyn --target=linux -o output
tayni-c input.tyn --target=wasm -o output.wasm
tayni-c input.tyn --target=qir -o output.qir
tayni-c input.tyn --target=ptx -o output.ptx

# With exports (quantum/GPU)
tayni-c quantum.tyn --target=qir --export=qasm
tayni-c gpu.tyn --target=ptx --export=opencl

# Use LLVM+Clang flow (optional)
tayni-c input.tyn --use-clang

# JSON output (for programmatic use)
tayni-c input.tyn --check --json
```

## Training Data
Location: docs/TAYNI-TRAINING-DATA.jsonl
Format: JSONL with input/output pairs
Count: 100+ examples

Example entry:
{"input": "Create HTTP server on port 8080", "output": "USE http\n.port: 8080\n.server: HTTP.LISTEN .port\n!"}

## Grammar
Location: docs/TAYNI-REFERENCE-v0.22.md
Format: EBNF

Key syntax:
- Bindings: .id: VALUE or .id: OPERATION args
- Imports: USE module_name
- Terminator: !

## Documentation
- docs/TAYNI-GUIDE-v0.22.md - Complete language guide
- docs/TAYNI-REFERENCE-v0.22.md - EBNF grammar
- docs/TAYNI-EXAMPLES-v0.22.md - Examples
- docs/TAYNI-SEMANTICS-v0.22.md - Type system
- docs/ARCHITECTURE.md - Compiler architecture
